Jeroboam and his people deserved the harsh punishment that Abijah was allowed to carry out on them because of their turning away from God and worshiping idols. It seems from what we know about Abijah that he was not truly religious himself, but he drew strength from the faith of his people. People who deny the power of true religion often talk proudly about its outward appearance. Many people who have little religion themselves value it in others. However, it was true that there were many faithful worshippers in Judah, and they had the more just cause. In their distress, when danger surrounded them, where should they turn for deliverance except to God? It is a great comfort that our path to God is always open. They cried out to the Lord. Earnest prayer is crying out. To their prayers, they added shouts of faith and became victorious. Jeroboam escaped Abijah’s sword, but God struck him; there is no escaping God's power.
